abstract: Attempts to transition the state machine from its current state to a state of the specified class.
overview:
## Return Value

[doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true] if the transition was successful; otherwise [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/false].

## Discussion

You build a state machine by creating a unique [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKState] subclass for each distinct state possible for the machine. The [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKState/isValidNextState(_:)] method of each state object determines which other states a state machine is allowed to transition into from that state. Calling this method first tests whether a transition from the current state to the specified state is valid; if not, this method returns [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/false].

If a transition is allowed, the state machine sends the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKState/willExit(to:)] message to its current state object. Then, the new state object replaces the value of the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKStateMachine/currentState] property. Finally, the state machine sends the  [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKState/didEnter(from:)] message to the new current state object, and this method returns [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true].

A newly created state machine’s [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKStateMachine/currentState] property is `nil`—to choose and enter an initial state, use the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKStateMachine/enter(_:)] method. In this case, the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKStateMachine/enter(_:)] call always succeeds.

# enter(_:)

**Instance Method**

Attempts to transition the state machine from its current state to a state of the specified class.

## Declaration

```swift
func enter(_ stateClass: AnyClass) -> Bool
```

## Parameters

- **stateClass**: The class of state into which to attempt a transition.

## Return Value

[doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true] if the transition was successful; otherwise [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/false].

## Discussion

You build a state machine by creating a unique [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKState] subclass for each distinct state possible for the machine. The [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKState/isValidNextState(_:)] method of each state object determines which other states a state machine is allowed to transition into from that state. Calling this method first tests whether a transition from the current state to the specified state is valid; if not, this method returns [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/false].

If a transition is allowed, the state machine sends the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKState/willExit(to:)] message to its current state object. Then, the new state object replaces the value of the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKStateMachine/currentState] property. Finally, the state machine sends the  [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKState/didEnter(from:)] message to the new current state object, and this method returns [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true].

A newly created state machine’s [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKStateMachine/currentState] property is `nil`—to choose and enter an initial state, use the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKStateMachine/enter(_:)] method. In this case, the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKStateMachine/enter(_:)] call always succeeds.

## Working with States

- **currentState**: The state machine’s current state.
- **canEnterState(_:)**: Returns a Boolean value indicating whether it is valid for the state machine to transition from its current state to a state of the specified class.
- **update(deltaTime:)**: Tells the current state object to perform per-frame updates.
